summary: Kernel P0s (#972, #973) resolved — stale state.json updated. Systemic CI failures across ALL 8 open PRs (test-and-build fails on 7/8, lint on 5/8) — likely Prettier regression cascading through branches. QA squad actively chasing the fix (#1121, #1123) but duplicate effort detected. Marketing still blocked by #995 (governance catch-22); HQ owns it but has no sprint goal. No squads at red. 3 squads idle (HQ, QA, Design) with no sprint goals.

kernel.sprintGoal: Fix P0 hook-breaking bugs (#972, #973), then resume Go kernel wire-up (#955)
kernel.notes: Both P0 blockers (#972, #973) are now CLOSED. State.json was stale — updated this run. 4298 tests all passing (+63 since last cycle). Senior dev slot unassigned — should resume Go kernel wire-up (#955). Kernel EM last ran 2026-03-26T09:00Z (33+ hours stale).
actionItems: Resume #955 (Go kernel wire-up) — assign senior slot
Trigger kernel EM run to refresh state

qa.notes: Two open PRs (#1121, #1123) both address Prettier regressions + go-fast-path flaky test. Duplicate effort — consolidate. Both have CI failures (test-and-build). Last run 2026-03-27T03:50Z produced 4298 passing tests locally, but CI disagrees.
actionItems: Investigate systemic test-and-build CI failure root cause

site.sprintGoal: Keep site numeric claims in sync with codebase
site.notes: PR #1117 (sync test count 2637→4161) open but CI failing (lint + test-and-build). Aligned with sprint goal.
actionItems: Fix CI failures on #1117, likely same root cause as systemic issue

marketing.sprintGoal: Unblock content agents and publish Go kernel launch content
marketing.notes: Blocked by #995 (governance catch-22: pnpm install blocked by hook before kernel exists). Owner listed as ops/hq-em but HQ is idle. This blocker has persisted across multiple director runs.
actionItems: Force-reassign #995 from HQ to kernel squad — they own the hook/governance path
Consider workaround: exempt pnpm install from governance during bootstrap

studio.sprintGoal: Ship agentguard init studio wizard + execution profiles + swarm template schema
studio.notes: Sprint goal largely SHIPPED in v2.7.x per ROADMAP.md. State not updated in 2 days. Needs new sprint goal assignment.
actionItems: Update state to reflect shipped work
Assign new sprint goal (Runner prep? Cloud protocol?)

hq.notes: No sprint goal, no assignments. Owns marketing blocker #995 but not acting on it. Last updated 2026-03-25.
actionItems: Assign sprint goal or merge HQ responsibilities into other squads

design.notes: No sprint goal, no assignments. Last updated 2026-03-25.

crossSquadIssues.title: Systemic CI failures across ALL open PRs
crossSquadIssues.description: All 8 open PRs have failing CI (test-and-build: 7/8 failing, lint: 5/8 failing). Root cause appears to be Prettier regressions — QA squad (#1121, #1123) is chasing the fix. This blocks all merges across all squads.

crossSquadIssues.title: Marketing blocker #995 (governance catch-22) — persisting across runs
crossSquadIssues.description: #995: pnpm install blocked by governance hook before kernel exists. Assigned to ops/hq-em but HQ is idle. Force-reassigning to kernel squad.

crossSquadIssues.title: Duplicate QA PRs (#1121 vs #1123)
crossSquadIssues.description: Both PRs address Prettier regressions and go-fast-path flaky test. #1123 is newer and includes AGENTGUARD_GO_TIMEOUT configurable. Close #1121.

strategicAlignment.notes: Go kernel Phase 1 fast-path delegation is shipped. Kernel squad should now resume #955 (Go wire-up). Framework adapters (DeepAgents #1126, OpenCode #1109) align with Phase 9 roadmap but both have CI failures and #1109 is do-not-merge. KE-2 (ActionContext) is the next critical v3.0 blocker — no squad assigned yet. Studio work shipped ahead of schedule. v3.0 release path: unblock CI → ship KE-2 → stranger test → user capture funnel.
gaps: KE-2 (ActionContext) — next v3.0 blocker — no squad assigned
v3.0 stranger test — not yet planned
